title: Ctenopteris heteromorpha
taxonomy: Ctenopteris heteromorpha belongs to the family Pteridaceae, which is a family of ferns commonly known as the wood ferns or tree ferns. This family is part of the order Cyatheales, which consists of tropical tree ferns. The order Cyatheales is further categorized under the class Polypodiopsida, which comprises the modern ferns. The class Polypodiopsida is then classified under the division Pteridophyta, which includes all vascular plants that reproduce via spores. Overall, Ctenopteris heteromorpha's classification places it within the plant kingdom, specifically within the group of vascular plants.

intro: Ctenopteris heteromorpha, also known as Hikosaka's maidenhair fern, is a species of fern native to New Zealand. The health benefits of Ctenopteris heteromorpha include its anti-inflammatory, antifungal, and antibacterial properties, which can aid in reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and sesquiterpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional New Zealand herbal medicine, Ctenopteris heteromorpha is often prepared as a tea or tincture to support respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of coughs and colds.
